label: KB Memory Type
description: Classifies a YAC_MEMORY record by the kind of fact it captures (CASE-507). Mirrors the four types in a YAC's local memory frontmatter: user (who Peter is / preferences), feedback (standing guidance on how to work), project (ongoing work / constraints not derivable from code), reference (pointers to external resources). Term-backed so the type is a controlled facet — discoverable via list_terms, filterable, and the axis a later gene-pool harvest step reasons over. Distinct from KB_DOC_KIND (which classifies markdown DOCUMENT mirrors).

terms.label: User
terms.description: Who the user (Peter) is — role, expertise, preferences. Constellation-applicable: the same Peter across every YAC, so the highest-value context to seed into a cold new YAC.

terms.label: Feedback
terms.description: Standing guidance on how the agent should work — corrections and confirmed approaches, with the why. The densest behavioural context a YAC accrues.

terms.label: Project
terms.description: Ongoing work, goals, or constraints not derivable from the code or git history. Relative dates converted to absolute.

terms.label: Reference
terms.description: Pointers to external resources — URLs, dashboards, tickets, gene-pool master locations.
